title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Oliver and Hercule Poirot discuss the possibility that a genuine codicil regarding Lady Llewellyn-Smith's estate might be hidden within a book titled "In Search of All Mysteries" at Stone Court, though most of the estate's contents have been sold. Mrs. Oliver questions whether Mrs. Lymon was coerced into lying and expresses interest in the disappearance of Olga. Poirot reveals that his assistant in London is gathering information and expects news from Bosnia, which may clarify if Olga has returned or provide access to letters she wrote to a confidante. Mrs. Oliver also mentions the unsolved strangulation of a schoolteacher previously noted by Elizabeth Whitaker.

Later, Poirot walks through a path in a stone quarry garden, noting that the path appears to have been widened by someone larger than Miranda Butler. He reflects on the garden's eerie, pagan atmosphere and remains certain that the will sent for examination is a forgery, reasoning that the lawyer Mr. Fullerton is too capable to suggest legal action without evidence. While walking, Poirot encounters Michael Garfield sketching in the garden and Miranda Butler standing by a nearby stream. Poirot observes Michael's skill at pencil sketching and engages him in conversation. Michael explains that he draws to record things before they disappear, specifically mentioning that he wishes to remember Miranda as a subject. Miranda approaches and claims her presence by the stream is a coincidence rather than a planned modeling session.
